title: Combat suit
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Armor |image=[[File:Combatsuit.jpg]] |name=Combat suit |manufacturer= |model= |type=[[Armor/Legends|Light armor]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |culture=*[[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] *[[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] *[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' |creators= |created= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners=*[[Jorj Car'das]] *[[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] |locations=[[Springhawk/Legends|''Springhawk'']] |cost= |value= |structure= |size= |color= |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight= |protection= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose= |heritage= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|How long until we get there?''"
"''Approximately four hours. In the meantime, I've had a combat suit prepared for you, one with more armoring and self-sealant capabilities than your suit from the ''Bargain Hunter''. Go below and put it on. The armorer will assist you.|Force Commander Mitth'raw'nuruodo, to Jorj Car'das|}} '''Combat suits''' were among the cheapest and most basic forms of body [[Armor/Legends|armor]] that was available in the decades before, during and after the [[Jedi Civil War]]. These types of suits were seen all over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and could be found for sale by almost any merchant who dealt in weapons and armor. Many such suits were used by military organizations, such as the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] military, as well as by [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], criminals, [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and even some [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. The suit itself offered decent protection from most types of attacks while maintaining maximum flexibility and minimum weight. However this armor was only recommended for light skirmishes. The [[Cruiser/Legends|Chiss attack cruiser]] [[Springhawk/Legends|''Springhawk'']] of the [[Picket Force Two/Legends|Picket Force Two]] had an armory with a stash of armored [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] combat suits with self-sealant capabilities. Prior to the [[Destruction of Special Task Force One]], [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], [[Core name/Legends|core name]] "Thrawn," of the [[Chiss Expansionary Fleet/Legends|Chiss Expansionary Fleet]] prepared a combat suit for the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jorj Car'das]]. Car'das and a Chiss [[Armorer/Legends|armorer]] spent three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] to get the suit fitted correctly, while spending the remaining fourth hour reviewing its characteristics. Jorj Car'das believed the Chiss combat suit was heavier than standard [[Environment suit/Legends|vac-suits]]. While Car'das returned to the ''Springhawk''{{'s}} bridge, he found Thrawn and the bridge crew had already donned their combat suits. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Allegiance (novel)|''Allegiance'']] {{Ab|Allegiance (audiobook)}} *''[[Choices of One]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'' {{1st}} *{{InsiderCite|75|Or Die Trying|reprint=1}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pl=Uniform bojowy }} [[Category:Light armor]]
